articles.description:
Meta paused work with Sama last month after allegations about staff viewing private scenes filmed by smart glasses
More than 1,000 low-paid workers in Kenya have been abruptly sacked by an outsourcing company contracted by Meta, in what activists said was a shocking move exposing the precariousness of tech jobs in the global south.
Sama, a company based in Nairobi to which Meta outsourced content moderation and AI training work, announced on Thursday that the workers were being laid off after Meta terminated a contract.

articles.title: Weather tracker: hail covers parts of Tunisia and Algeria like snow
articles.description:
Accumulations of up to 3cm deep reported as severe thunderstorms also bring heavy downpours to central Italy
Severe thunderstorms have affected the Mediterranean this week. On Monday, a surface low-pressure system in the Mediterranean in conjunction with an upper air cut-off low, led to thunderstorms over north Africa. Their intensity was aided by the hot precursor conditions.
Algeria and Tunisia were notably affected by the thunderstorms, with some hail accumulation layers as a result. When so much hail forms, it starts to lay down sheets of hail, covering the ground like snow. Hail accumulations of up to 3cm were reported in Oum Ladjoul and Hammam Sokhna in Algeria, and there were hailstones of up to 3cm in diameter in Makthar, Tunisia. Thunderstorms continued in the region through the following day, with further hail accumulations, notably in Ouled Bousmir, Tunisia, where there was a layer about 2cm deep.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: South African politician Julius Malema given five-year jail term for gun offence

Leader of leftwing Economic Freedom Fighters was convicted last year for firing rifle in the air at 2018 rally
The South African leftwing politician Julius Malema has been sentenced to five years in prison for firing a rifle in the air at a political rally in 2018.
Lawyers for the leader of the Economic Freedom Fighters, South Africa’s fourth largest political party, immediately appealed, and Malema will remain free while the appeal proceedings are under way.

articles.title: More than £1bn pledged for Sudan as humanitarian crisis deepens

Donors exceed funding target at Berlin conference but prospects for ceasefire remain distant
More than £1bn (€1.15bn) has been pledged for war-ravaged Sudan at a conference in Berlin, eclipsing the funding target organisers had set to help mitigate the world’s largest humanitarian crisis.
The financial commitments made on Wednesday will also help offset a chronic humanitarian funding shortfall in a country devastated by three years of conflict, where two-thirds of its population – 34m people – require assistance.

articles.title: Suspicion surrounds death of US influencer Ashly Robinson in Zanzibar
articles.description:
Lifestyle influencer died while on vacation with boyfriend, who local officials say has since had his passport ‘withheld’
Ashly Robinson, a US lifestyle influencer, died last week while on vacation in the Tanzanian islands of Zanzibar with her boyfriend, Joe McCann. Robinson’s death on 9 April, just days after her birthday and a marriage proposal from McCann, has sparked suspicion on social media, with users doubtful of the current narrative surrounding her death.
No arrests have been made, and police previously said that McCann was not suspected of wrongdoing. But officials in Zanzibar released a statement on Tuesday saying that McCann’s passport has been “withheld”.

“Venezuela will be free,” the Nobel peace prize winner insisted in an interview on the eve of this weekend’s demonstration in the Puerta del Sol square, which is expected to draw tens of thousands of protesters.

articles.title: Air Canada temporarily suspends some flights to New York and other locations
articles.description:
Spirit Airlines reportedly seeks emergency US government funding as war against Iran keeps aviation fuel costs high

Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

Air Canada has announced a temporary suspension of flights from Toronto and Montreal to New York’s John F Kennedy airport, citing rising fuel prices.
The move comes amid growing concerns that airlines worldwide may scale back services as aviation fuel costs climb in the wake of the US and Israel’s ongoing war with Iran, which entered a fragile ceasefire earlier in April. Although Iran announced on Friday that the strait of Hormuz had reopened, helping ease oil prices, fuel costs remain significantly elevated after weeks of disruption.

The progressive Mexican leader has walked a careful line with Trump for more than a year, addressing provocations with a measured tone and meeting US requests to crack down on cartels more so than her predecessors, in an effort to offset threats of tariffs and US military action against gangs.

articles.titleJa: イラン、ホルムズ海峡の「厳格な管理」再開を発表

articles.title: La coup de Grasset: The exodus of authors and the choice of a collective response
articles.description: Not wanting to drift with their publishing house after Olivier Nora's ouster, 170 authors have announced that they are leaving Grasset. Writer Oscar Coop-Phane reflects on his experience. Bookseller Gwilherm Perthuis denounces an ultra-concentrated publishing industry.
articles.titleJa: グラッセ社のクーデター：著者らの集団脱退と連帯による対応
articles.summary: オリヴィエ・ノラの追放後、グラッセ社から170人の著者が脱退を発表。出版業界の集中化に対する抗議を反映している。

articles.title: Death penalty law proves the EU must treat Israel as an apartheid state
articles.description: Pernando Barrena says Israel’s death penalty for Palestinians adds to the case that Europe should cut ties with it.
articles.titleJa: 死刑法がEUにイスラエルをアパルトヘイト国家として扱うべきであることを証明する
articles.summary: イスラエルがパレスチナ人に対する死刑法を導入し、EUは同国をアパルトヘイト国家として扱い、関係を断つべきだという主張が展開されている。

articles.title: Pakistan PM, army chief wrap up key trips in push for more US-Iran talks
articles.description: Asim Munir leaves Tehran while PM Shehbaz Sharif heads home from Turkiye with hopes of another round of US-Iran talks.
articles.titleJa: パキスタン首相と陸軍参謀長、米国・イラン間の更なる協議を推進する主要な訪問を終える

articles.title: Iran has learned that the Strait of Hormuz is its strongest deterrent
articles.description: Donald Heflin, a senior fellow at Tufts University, says Iran has shown it doesn’t need nuclear weapons as a deterrent.
articles.titleJa: イランはホルムズ海峡がその最強の抑止力であることを学んだ
articles.summary: 専門家は、イランがホルムズ海峡を最強の抑止力として認識しており、核兵器なしでも戦略的影響力を持つことを示していると指摘。

articles.title: Man City vs Arsenal: All you need to know about title showdown
articles.description: Under-pressure Arsenal head to in-form Manchester City as their stranglehold on Premier League title race falters.
articles.titleJa: マンチェスター・シティ対アーセナル：タイトル決定戦について知っておくべきことすべて
articles.summary: プレッシャー下のアーセナルが好調なマンチェスター・シティと対戦。プレミアリーグのタイトル争いの行方を左右する大一番の詳細を解説。

articles.title: Pope Leo heads to Angola in landmark Africa visit amid Trump clash
articles.description: Leo is the third pontiff to visit the fossil fuel-rich country after John Paul II in 1992 and Benedict XVI in 2009.
articles.titleJa: レオ教皇、トランプ氏との対立のさなか、歴史的なアフリカ訪問でアンゴラへ向かう
articles.summary: レオ教皇がアンゴラを訪問。歴代教皇に続き3度目のアフリカ訪問で、トランプ氏との対立報道の中、重要な意味合いを持つとされる。

articles.title: Israeli police destroy children’s footballs at Al-Aqsa mosque
articles.description: Israeli police confiscated and destroyed footballs that were being played with by children around Al-Aqsa mosque.
articles.titleJa: イスラエル警察、アルアクサ・モスクで子供たちのサッカーボールを破壊
articles.summary: イスラエル警察が、アルアクサ・モスク周辺で遊んでいた子供たちのサッカーボールを押収・破壊。この行為は地域の緊張関係を象徴している。

articles.title: Trump says US will ‘start dropping bombs again’ if no Iran deal is reached
articles.description: US President Donald Trump says the US will “have to start dropping bombs again” if no US-Iran deal is reached.
articles.titleJa: トランプ氏、イランとの合意不成立なら米国は「再び爆弾を落とし始める」と発言
articles.summary: トランプ米大統領は、イランとの合意が成立しない場合、米国が再び爆撃を開始せざるを得なくなると発言し、国際情勢に波紋を広げている。

articles.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 50 of the US-Iran conflict?
articles.description: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz again until US lifts its ports blockade, hours after Trump said he was optimistic of deal.
articles.titleJa: イラン戦争：米イラン紛争50日目に何が起こっているのか？
articles.summary: 米イラン紛争50日目。イランは、米国が港湾封鎖を解除するまでホルムズ海峡を再び閉鎖。トランプ氏の楽観的発言直後の出来事だ。

articles.title: Curry, Warriors knocked out of NBA play-in tournament by Suns

articles.titleJa: カリーとウォリアーズ、サンズにNBAプレーイントーナメントで敗退

articles.title: Al-Noor centre: A lifeline for blind children in Gaza

articles.titleJa: アル・ヌール・センター：ガザの盲目の子供たちの命綱
articles.summary: ガザにあるUNRWAのアル・ヌール・センターは、視覚障害を持つ子供たちに教育と支援を提供。紛争地域で彼らにとって重要な希望の場となっている。

articles.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz is 'open' but tracking shows few ships moving

articles.titleJa: イラン、ホルムズ海峡は「開いている」と主張も、追跡調査では船の動きは少ない

articles.title: A moment of risk and opportunity: How two ceasefires could boost US-Iran talks

articles.summary: 二つの停戦が、米国とイランの間の外交交渉を促進する可能性を秘めている。戦争再開を防ぐための外交的進展が見られるが、それが十分であるかは疑問視されている。

articles.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire

articles.summary: イスラエルとレバノンの間で10日間の停戦が発効し、イラン支援のヒズボラも支持を表明した。米国とイランの間でも交渉が続いている。

articles.title: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
articles.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.
articles.titleJa: ライーズ・ドゥーセット：脆い停戦下、イラン国民は米国との合意が可能か疑問視
articles.summary: 脆弱な停戦下で、イラン国民は米国との合意が実現可能か懐疑的だ。戦争再開回避に向けた外交努力が強化される中、BBCの特派員がイランから報告する。

articles.title: Satellite images reveal scale of Israeli demolitions as Lebanese villages destroyed

articles.title: Israeli triple-tap strike kills three paramedics in Lebanon, officials say

articles.titleJa: イスラエルによる三連空爆でレバノンの救急隊員3人が死亡と当局発表

articles.title: Prominent Palestinian prisoner Marwan Barghouti assaulted three times in a month, family says

articles.titleJa: 著名なパレスチナ人囚人、マルワン・バルグーティが1カ月に3度暴行を受けたと家族が主張

articles.title: Israel and Hezbollah continue attacks after Israel-Lebanon talks in US

articles.titleJa: 米国のイスラエル・レバノン協議後も攻撃続く、イスラエルとヒズボラ

articles.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 Iranian sailors stranded after US torpedo attack

articles.titleJa: スリランカ、米魚雷攻撃で遭難したイラン人船員238人を本国送還

articles.title: Lebanon seeks peace, but Hezbollah needs to be convinced first

articles.title: Zimbabwe's iconic stone birds were taken by colonialists. Finally, they're all back home

articles.title: What next for South African opposition firebrand Malema after his five-year prison sentence?

articles.title: 'I was tortured and lost my hand' - one student's struggle to get an education in Nigeria

articles.title: Pope lashes out at foreigners who exploit Africa

articles.titleJa: ローマ教皇、アフリカを搾取する外国人に対し激しく非難

articles.title: Pope prioritises world's fastest-growing Catholic region in major Africa tour

articles.title: The prophet and the mysterious death of Charmain Speirs

articles.title: Plan to scrap presidential elections puts Zimbabweans at loggerheads

articles.title: Carnival fever hits Lagos as locals celebrate Afro-Brazilian heritage

articles.title: When climate change impairs beekeeping in the Democratic Republic of the Congo

articles.title: When technology fails women: Online abuse and Nigeria’s digital weak points
articles.description: When AI-generated harassment is treated as “engaging content,” it gets amplified, and women, especially women from the Global majority, become collateral damage.

articles.title: DRC: Urban trees are natural CO₂ stores that merit closer attention

articles.title: How initiatives to narrow Africa’s digital gap are affecting women’s representation

articles.title: Cameroonian rice farmers caught between national ambition and rural realities

articles.title: African women make economic gains through global gig economy

articles.title: UK seeks closer EU ties in volatile times - but at what cost?

articles.title: Europe has 'maybe six weeks of jet fuel left', energy boss warns

articles.description: The WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14) concluded early 30 March with ministers adopting a number of decisions as well as making a commitment to continue work in Geneva on key outstanding issues.
articles.titleJa: 第14回閣僚会議、決定採択と主要未解決問題での進展をもって閉幕
articles.summary: WTO第14回閣僚会議（MC14）は、複数の決定を採択し、主要な未解決問題についてジュネーブで作業を続けることを約束して閉幕した。

articles.title: DG cites MPIA as "practical, confidence-building bridge" pending dispute reform deal
articles.description: The Multi-Party Interim Appeal Arbitration Arrangement (MPIA) is serving as a practical, confidence building bridge for WTO members committed to a fully functioning dispute settlement system pending an agreement on dispute settlement reform, Director-General Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala said on 28 March. She made her remarks at a meeting of parties to the MPIA on the sidelines of the WTO's 14th Ministerial Conference (MC14).
